prose:
**Furan frontier-orbital result (computed)** — Property: Kohn–Sham HOMO–LUMO gap. Level of theory: DFT tier `standard`, explicitly `b3lyp/def2-tzvp`, run with PySCF. Geometry: user-supplied input geometry at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/furan/furan.xyz`; frontier orbitals were computed for that input geometry. Environment: gas phase (`solvent: null`). Charge 0, multiplicity 1.

**Headline result:** HOMO–LUMO gap = **6.2573602 eV**.

Additional orbital energies returned by chemkit: HOMO = -6.3597638 eV; LUMO = -0.1024036 eV. Total electronic energy = -230.11429 hartree (-6261.7289 eV). SCF converged: true, in 9 cycles. Atom count in input: 9.

Warnings: no warnings reported in the tool result.

Integrity verdict: `integrity.trustworthy = true`.

Reproducibility: chemkit skill used: `frontier-orbitals`; engine subcommand returned in provenance: `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/furan/furan.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 --accept-defaults --stdout path --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpalrzy3l7/live.json`. The chemkit tool did not return a separate live `.out` log path in the result JSON I received, so I cannot quote one without guessing.
